data.title: E-commerce accelerator Pattern Group begins trading slightly below the IPO pricing level
data.description:
Pattern Group (NASDAQ:PTRN) opened for trading early Friday afternoon at $13.50 before settling back to $13.39 at 1:10 p.m. The IPO was priced at $14.00, which was the middle of the anticipated range. Co-founder and CEO Mel Alder rang the opening bell on the Nasdaq earlier in the day.

Pattern Group was originally founded as iServe in 2013 in Lehi, Utah, by David Wright and Melanie Alder. By 2018, the company rebranded from iServe to Pattern and expanded internationally with the acquisition of the consultancy Practicology.

The Utah-based company uses advanced AI and machine learning to manage logistics, pricing, advertising, and customer service for its partners. Its specialty is managing branded sales on digital marketplaces like Amazon (AMZN), Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), and TikTok Shop

Pattern Group (NASDAQ:PTRN) disclosed that it generated revenue of $1.8 billion for the year that ended on December 31, 2024, which represents a CAGR of 35% over the last two years. During the same period, the company's net income improved from -$3 million to $68 million. Adjusted EBITDA during 2024 was $101 million, which represented a CAGR of 138% over the prior two years.

Notably, Pattern Group (PTRN) derived 94% of its revenue from consumer product sales on Amazon (AMZN). In addition, Amazon (AMZN) accounted for over 75% of the total U.S. marketplace retail sales in 2024. For the first six months of 2025, Pattern Group (PTRN) reported a 35% year-over-year jump in revenue.

E-commerce players that Pattern Group (PTRN) competes with include MarketLeap, Jodaro, Assiduus Global, Spreetail, Quince, Dianxiaomi, CloudCommerce, Trend, Shopify (SHOP), and Texture.

data.title: Capstone Copper resumes full operations at Mantoverde mine
data.description:
Capstone Copper (OTCPK:CSCCF) said late Thursday it resumed full sulphide copper production at its Mantoverde mine in Chile, following repairs to a ball mill motor that had interrupted production since late August.

Capstone (OTCPK:CSCCF) reported on September 1 that a second motor failure had curbed output at Mantoverde, reducing production by 3,000-4,000 tons of copper concentrate while repairs were completed; the mine was forced to operate by bypassing one of its two ball mills after the breakdown on August 30.

The company said at the time it would mitigate the impact by advancing planned maintenance, while also preparing a long-term replacement and repair strategy.

Five days of planned maintenance are still expected to be conducted at the end of September, the company said.

data.title: Judge dismisses Trump's $15B NYT lawsuit for violating court rules
data.description:
A Florida judge on Friday temporarily dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump's $15B lawsuit against The New York Times (NYSE:NYT) for being too long and violating court rules.

According to U.S. District Judge Steven Merryday, Trump's lawyers did not follow the court rules that require "short, plain, direct" statements outlining why the plaintiff deserves relief.

However, the judge said that his lawyers can file an amended suit by next month.

The judge claimed that the Trump lawsuit "unmistakably and inexcusably" violated federal court rules by presenting allegations that were "repetitive," "superfluous," and "florid." 

Judge Merriday also said that his complaint is not “a public forum for vituperation and invective” or “a protected platform to rage against an adversary.”

Trump had launched the lawsuit against the newspaper earlier this week and accused it of being a mouthpiece for the Democratic Party and for having an agenda against him.

He accused the paper of having "engaged in decades long method of lying" and pointed to concerns such as the Times’ endorsement of former presidential candidate Kamala Harris.

NYT shares were up marginally and did not have a significant reaction to the news.

The lawsuit is President Donald J. Trump v. New York Times Company, case no. 8:25-cv-02487, filed in the United States District Court, Middle District of Florida, Tampa Division.  

More on New York Times

data.title: Lowe's Companies to acquire ASP Flag Parent Holdings for ~$8.8B
data.description:
Lowe's Companies (NYSE:LOW) said in an SEC filing on Friday that it will acquire all the shares of Delaware corporation, ASP Flag Parent Holdings, for ~$8.8B.

The home improvement company has entered into a credit agreement for a $2B unsecured revolving loan facility to replace its existing $2B unsecured revolving credit facility.

Also, the company has signed a term loan credit agreement for a $2B unsecured term loan facility. Additionally, Lowe's said an aggregate amount of up to $5B in bridge facility commitments remain.

data.title: Maersk leads container shippers down as freight rates plunge to nearly two-year lows
data.description:
Shipping companies A.P. Moller-Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) and Hapag-Lloyd (OTCPK:HPGLY) (OTCPK:HLAGF) -6.6% and -7.5%, respectively, in Friday's trading as container freight rates on major routes out of Shanghai sink to levels last seen before Red Sea diversions began in late 2023.

The Shanghai Containerized Freight Index has plunged more than 14% in the past week, the largest weekly percentage decline in nine years. according to the Shanghai Shipping Exchange.

The week's biggest declines have occurred on the TransPacific trade: Spot rates from Shanghai to the U.S. West Coast plummeted 31% to $1,646/feu, and rates from Shanghai to the U.S. East Coast plunged 23% to $2,557/feu.

Rates from Asia to Europe also continue to decline, with rates to north Europe and the Mediterranean both down more than 10% to $1,052/teu and $1,638/teu, respectively.

"Far East-to-U.S. west coast rates are now down 56% from its recent peak reached in week 23 and 61% down from week 1," J.P. Morgan analysts said, and maritime research consultancy Drewry said in a note that its world container index, which tracks freight rates for 40 ft containers on eight major shipping routes, shows the major Trans Pacific and Asia-Europe routes are now "aligned in a downwards trajectory."

The declines come ahead of Golden Week that traditionally marks the start of shipping's slow season, which analysts said offer a bleak prospect for rates in the coming weeks.

data.title: BAM-backed Rockpoint Gas Storage files for IPO in Canada - report
data.description:
Rockpoint Gas Storage, the North American natural gas storage platform backed by Brookfield Asset Management (NYSE:BAM), has filed for a Canadian initial public offering, according to a media report on Friday.

The Toronto listing will comprise new shares and existing stock, Bloomberg reported, citing a preliminary prospectus. The IPO price and number of shares offered will be disclosed in subsequent filings.

Brookfield (NYSE:BAM) will maintain control after the offering, the article said, keeping some 60% of the operating units and all the Class B shares. Rockpoint was incorporated this year to buy about 40% of two controlled operating partnerships controlled by Brookfield.

The filing showed that Rockpoint's net income and revenue were $209.4M and $415.3M, respectively, in the fiscal year ended March 31. That compares with $253.9M of net income and $348.6M of revenue in the prior 12 months.

data.title: Kohl's added to Citi's 90 day positive catalyst watch
data.description:
Citi Research has placed Kohl’s Corporation (NYSE:KSS) on Positive Catalyst Watch as improved comparable store sales comparisons in the second half of this year and an acceleration in web traffic suggests the consensus expectations for the department store are “overly conservative.”

“While the second half is likely to present macro challenges, and longer-term secular issues remain, we believe that near-term comp upside combined with the company’s high short-interest (~30%) should move the stock higher in the near term,” Citi analyst Lejuez writes in Friday’s note to clients.

Lejuez cites Citi’s analysis which indicates H2 comparisons are ~325 basis points easier than in H1, suggesting the consensus forecast of -4.2% in comparable sales in H2 appears overdone.

Furthermore, web traffic data indicates an acceleration to +9.4% in the third quarter versus -7.8% in the second quarter.

Accordingly, Citi Research raised its FY25 EPS estimate to $0.96 from $0.86 and Q3 EPS forecast to a loss of $0.10 from a loss of $0.21.

The firm also lifted its price target for Kohl’s (NYSE:KSS) by 19% to $19.

data.title: Walmart faces questions linking lax third-party vetting to marketplace counterfeits -- report
data.description:
Shares of Walmart (NYSE:WMT) are on watch in the wake of a CNBC report alleging that the retail giant’s lax vetting process for third-party merchants has resulted in a proliferation of counterfeit products on its website.

A CNBC investigation into Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) marketplace uncovered “dozens” of third-party sellers who had stolen the identity of another business,” and subsequently sold fake health and beauty products online.

The network included an interview with a Walmart employee responsible for approving third-party applications. According to the employee, Walmart (NYSE:WMT) was initially strict on third-party applications, but over time pressured employees to relax their standards and approve more sellers due to increased competition from Amazon (AMZN).

She added that sellers who were frustrated with Amazon’s (AMZN) rigorous screening moved to Walmart (NYSE:WMT), which allowed them to list their products more easily. Data compiled by Marketplace Pulse found that during the period between 2019 and 2024, the number of sellers on Walmart’s marketplace exploded by 900%, overlapping with a period in which Amazon (AMZN) increased security controls and banned many sellers for selling counterfeits.

By accepting more risks, Walmart’s (WMT) U.S. e-commerce business has swelled to nearly $100B in annual sales, CNBC claims. “They wanted the business, so they were willing to take a chance on it,” the employee said to CNBC.

In a statement to CNBC, Walmart (WMT) said it enforces a “zero-tolerance policy for prohibited or noncompliant products and continues to invest in new tools and technologies to help ensure only trusted, legitimate items reach our customers.”

However, as part of its investigation into Walmart (WMT) and Amazon’s (AMZN) seller applications, CNBC found that Walmart (WMT) required less documentation and vetting to sign up for its marketplace and imposed fewer restrictions on the types of products that could be sold compared to its competitors like Amazon (AMZN). The network tested the authenticity of 20 items offered by third-party sellers that were impersonating legitimate businesses and found all 20 were counterfeit.

Shares of Walmart (WMT) are down nearly 1% matching a move across the consumer staples sector (XLP). ETFs with an exposure to Walmart (WMT) include iShares Global Consumer Staples (KXI), Global X Purecap MSCI Consumer Staples (GXPS), and  Vanguard Consumer Staples Index Fund (VDC).

Walmart (WMT) has not yet responded to Seeking Alpha’s request for comment.
